searchDocs.title: For​
searchDocs.content: </> Delay:For( Value: any, Time: number ) → () Sets a custom wait time for a value

searchDocs.title: SetTo
searchDocs.content: SetTo A class that sets values.

searchDocs.title: Configure
searchDocs.content: Configure Allows configuration on an existing physical or none physical component. Inherits method from Create.

searchDocs.title: SetId​
searchDocs.content: </> Task:SetId(Id: string) → () Sets a unique ID for the task. This ID can be used to identify the task later.

searchDocs.title: Connect​
searchDocs.content: </> Task:Connect() → () Connects a signal to the task. The task will be called when the signal is fired.

searchDocs.title: AddParameter​
searchDocs.content: </> Task:AddParameter() → () Cleans up the task and removes it from the Space. This will also disconnect any connections made to the task.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Task:Destroy() → () Cleans up the task and removes it from the Space. This will also disconnect any connections made to the task.

searchDocs.title: Call​
searchDocs.content: </> Task:Call() → () Executes the task.

searchDocs.title: Call​
searchDocs.content: </> Space:Call() → () Calls all tasks in the Space.

searchDocs.title: Add​
searchDocs.content: </> Space:Add( Params: {[any]: any}, Thread: Thread? ) → () Adds/Creates a task to the Space. The task will be called when the Space is called.

searchDocs.title: Remove​
searchDocs.content: </> Space:Remove(Task: Task) → () Removes a task from the Space. The task will no longer be called when the Space is called.

searchDocs.title: Connect​
searchDocs.content: </> Space:Connect( Connection: RBXScriptSignal , Id: string? ) → () Connects a signal to space, pass an id if u only want that connection to fire a specific task.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Space:Destroy() → () Cleans up the Space and removes all tasks. This will also disconnect any connections made to the Space.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> DelayValue:Destroy() → () Destroys the delay value object and cleans up its resources.

searchDocs.content: </> DelayValue:SetTime(New: number) → () Sets the delay time.

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> DelayValue:Get() → any Returns the current value.

searchDocs.title: Refresh​
searchDocs.content: </> DelayValue:Refresh() → () Triggers the changed signal.

searchDocs.title: SetSignalEnabled​
searchDocs.content: </> DelayValue:SetSignalEnabled(Enabled: boolean) → ()

searchDocs.title: Sensitive​
searchDocs.content: </> DelayValue:Sensitive() → DelayValue Marks the delay value as sensitive, meaning it won't send a signal if another update is in queue.

searchDocs.title: ResetTime​
searchDocs.content: </> DelayValue:ResetTime() → () Resets the delay time to the default time.

searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Create( ClassName: string, Thread: {Add: ( self: {}, Entity: any ) → ()}? ) → (props: PropertyTypes.Property) → Create Creates a new instance object.

searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Info( Time: number?, EasingStyle: Enum.EasingStyle?, EasingDirection: Enum.EasingDirection?, RepeatCount: number?, Reverse: boolean?, DelayTime: number? ) → Info Creates a new Info object with the given parameters.

searchDocs.title: Extend​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Extend(NoLink: boolean) → Thread Creates a sub-thread. Leave the NoLink to nil and the thread will be linked to the parent thread(will delete with parent), set NoLink to true for the thread to be independent.

searchDocs.title: Spawn​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Spawn() → () Similar to Task.spawn, not to be used within a compileable table.

searchDocs.title: Delay​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Delay() → () Similar to Task.Delay, not to be used within a compileable table.

searchDocs.title: Debris​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Debris() → () Similar to game.debris:AddItem, not to be used within a compileable table.

searchDocs.title: Clean​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Clean() → () This method cleans everything added to the thread, and the thread itself. After this method gets called the thread is no longer useable.

searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Connect( Connection: RBXScriptSignal , Function:: () → () ) → () Signal connection

searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Add(Entity: any) → () The entity to add to the thread

searchDocs.title: Remove​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Remove(Entity: any) → () The entity to remove from the thread, meaning when the thread is cleaned it won't be cleaned with it.

searchDocs.title: Schedule​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Schedule( Time: number?, Item: any, ...: any ) → item Cleans an item individually based off the time. can also be used to call functions after a time has passed.

searchDocs.title: Listener​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Listener( Entity: any, Event(s): any, Function: function, Thread: any? ) → () Connects the given function to the specified event(s) on the entity.

searchDocs.title: MethodSignaled​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:MethodSignaled( Method: string, Index: string, Thread: Thread? ) → () Attribute changed listener

searchDocs.title: Space​
searchDocs.content: </> Thread:Space( func: function, Thread: Thread? ) → () Creates a new Space object. The space will call the function when the space is called.
